title: Duke Energy to fund new Jules Accelerator clean-tech camp for students
text: Student interns will help clean-tech startups expand programs into the community Joules Accelerator has been fostering energy startups for a decade CHARLOTTE, N.C. , June 30, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- The Duke Energy Foundation will fund a program that will allow college students to help energy startups – working with local neighborhoods to bring decarbonization programs to underserved communities in the Carolinas.

title: New South Carolina law could help electric customers save millions of dollars spent to restore power after unprecedented storm damage
text: Bipartisan effort gives Public Service Commission of South Carolina authority to approve plans that lower overall costs to customers. Coastal states including Texas, Louisiana, Florida, North Carolina have enacted similar securitization legislation.

title: 3 Safest Stocks on the Planet to Buy and Hold Forever
text: Thinking about playing defense? Here are some names that will hold up in a recession.

title: AES Sets Up US Solar Buyer Consortium to Promote Solar Industry
text: AES sets up the U.S. Solar Buyer Consortium to boost the growth of the American solar industry.

title: This Sector With Dependable, Big Dividends Likely Will Dodge the Bear Market
text: The liquidity party punch bowl is gone and will not be returning any time soon. Profligate and wasteful government spending, and way too much liquidity as rates were kept near or at zero for years, has created an inflation monster, and a Federal Reserve that is way behind the curve had no choice but come out blasting this month.

title: Duke Energy Progress submits annual update for fuel, renewable energy, energy efficiency program charges in North Carolina
text: Rising demand following COVID-19 shutdown paired with rising fuel costs, which Duke Energy does not profit on, will increase customer prices. If approved, customers will see bills increase starting in December.

title: Duke Energy Carolinas customers set summertime record for electricity use
text: Duke Energy Carolinas achieves new summer peak usage record: 21,086 megawatt-hours. Duke Energy Carolinas and Duke Energy Progress set new combined usage record: 34,079 megawatt-hours.

title: Largest floating solar power plant in the Southeast at Fort Bragg
text: Part of larger energy services contract with Duke Energy Project also includes a 2-MW battery   CHARLOTTE, N.C. , June 13, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- The Southeast's largest floating solar plant will be producing power soon at the U.S. Army's Fort Bragg in North Carolina after a major utility energy service contract with Duke Energy and its prime contractor Ameresco.

title: Duke Energy Florida connects customers to cleaner energy options with completion of first community solar site in Hardee County
text: Facility is first of 10 solar sites that are part of company's new community solar program. Enrolled income-qualified customers will see guaranteed savings on their program costs every month.

title: Duke Energy (DUK) Arm Rolls Out GreenEdge in North Carolina
text: Duke Energy's (DUK) subsidiary, Piedmont Natural Gas, introduces the GreenEdge program to enable its customers to buy green blocks voluntarily from it, with each block at $3.

title: Duke Energy programs offer relief from high cooling costs this summer
text: CHARLOTTE, N.C. , June 9, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- The warm days of summer are here, which means it is a prime time for a combination of outdoor activities and the cool comfort of indoors.

title: Piedmont Natural Gas introduces carbon-reducing program for North Carolina customers
text: GreenEdgeSM program allows customers to purchase blocks representing blend of renewable natural gas environmental attributes and carbon offsets. Customers can participate in program beginning at $3 per month.

title: Duke Energy (DUK) Starts Building 120-MW Solar Plant in Idaho
text: Duke Energy's (DUK) unit starts construction of the Jackpot Solar project, which is projected to commence commercial operation by the end of 2022

title: Duke Energy boosts Indiana's workforce readiness with more than $330,000 in training grants
text: Funding will support workforce development, educational programs statewide, including in Bartholomew, Clark, Clinton, Dearborn, Hamilton, Huntington, Jefferson, Johnson, Lawrence, Monroe, Owen, Putnam, Tippecanoe, Vanderburgh counties. PLAINFIELD, Ind.

title: Duke Energy (DUK) Increases Hydroelectric Plant Output by 10%
text: Duke Energy's (DUK) subsidiary enhances clean energy output by 10% at Markland Hydroelectric Station, located near Florence, IN.

title: Duke Energy One, L3Harris offer new operational solution for nuclear power plants
text: Innovative service for nuclear facilities provides customized, immersive 3D simulations. Solution provides way for customers to address operational opportunities and challenges, while improving efficiency, reducing costs.

title: More than $225,000 still available in energy bill assistance for Duke Energy Indiana customers
text: Qualifying customers can receive up to $300 toward their energy bills. PLAINFIELD, Ind.

title: Duke Energy supports fellow first responders in South Carolina with more than $500,000 in grants to support emergency preparedness
text: Funding will support 34 nonprofits, government agencies across state. Grants up to $20,000 will pay for training, life-saving equipment, new technology to aid in disaster planning, recovery operations.
